summary: Kenneth demonstrates a weekly covered call selling strategy targeting $180/week premium. He reviews 5 positions (HIMS, Joby, NPHASE, OPEN, QBTS), discusses timing mistakes made by selling calls too early in a down week, and demonstrates rolling expired calls out one week to capture higher premium as stocks recovered.
takeaways: Sell covered calls with 1-week expiration for maximum premium capture
Roll calls out 1 week when underlying stock price rises above strike price (keeps same strike for highest premium)
Avoid selling premium too early in volatile weeks; wait for recovery to sell at higher strikes/prices
Track cost basis and premium per position: NPHASE $7,500 cost for $110 premium (1.5%), OPEN $5,000 cost for $146 premium (2.9%)
Rolling mechanics: Buy back expired call at loss, immediately sell new 1-week call at same strike price to net additional premium
HIMS position: sold $49 strike call for $70 premium when stock closed at $48 (near ATM)
Weekly portfolio target: $180-$950+ range depending on rolling activity and stock recovery timing

strategy_candidates.change: Implement minimum premium threshold rules—do not sell covered calls during market downturns; instead queue positions and wait 2-3 days for recovery before selling, maximizing strike selection rather than forcing entry for small premiums
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly states 'I should have just sat on the sidelines' and 'I could have generated way more in premium' if he had waited through the Tuesday-Wednesday downturn. He recovered an extra $254 on OPEN and $265 on NPHASE by rolling instead of accepting the initial 1% weeks returns. This implies a rule like 'no premium sales on down-days; queue and execute on recovery day' would improve Brian's $1,250/mo target.

title: Part 14 9/2/25 $180 per week rolling calls
